title: Motion In a Straight Line Test - 5 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: A boy standing in an elevator accelerating upward throws a ball upward with a velocity $v_0$. The ball returns in his hands after a time $t$. The acceleration of the lift is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A stone is thrown upwards from a tower with a velocity $50\,ms^{-1}$. Another stone is simultaneously thrown downwards from the same location with a velocity $50\; ms^{-1}$. When the first stone is at the highest point, the relative velocity of the second stone with respect to the first stone is (assume that second stone has not yet reached the ground) :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Shown in the figure are the position time graph for two children going home from the school. Which of the following statements about their relative motion is true after both of them started moving? Their relative velocity : (consider 1-D motion)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Shown in the figure are the velocity time graphs of the two particles $P_1$ and $P_2$. Which of the following statements about their relative motion is true? Magnitude of their relative velocity : (consider 1-D motion)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two trains A and B which are 100 km apart are travelling towards each other on different tracks with each having initial speed of 50 km/h. The train A accelerates at $20\ \mathrm{km/h^2}$ and the train B retards at the rate $20\ \mathrm{km/h^2}$. The distance covered by the train A when they cross each other is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two cars are moving in the same direction with a speed of $30 \; \text{km h}^{-1}$. They are separated from each other by $5 \; \text{km}$. Third car moving in the opposite direction meets the two cars after an interval of 4 minutes. What is the speed of the third car?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A bus is moving with a velocity $10 \text{ ms}^{-1}$ on a straight road. A scooterist wishes to overtake the bus in $100$ s. If, the bus is at a distance of $1$ km from the scooterist, with what velocity should the scooterist chase the bus? (Neglect size of the bus)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A coin is released inside a lift at a height of 2 m from the floor of the lift. The height of the lift is 10 m. The lift is moving with an acceleration of $11\;\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^2$ downwards. The time after which the coin will strike with the lift is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A police van moving on a highway with a speed of $30\,\text{kmh}^{-1}$ fires a bullet at a thief's car speeding away in the same direction with a speed of $192\,\text{kmh}^{-1}$. If the muzzle speed of the bullet is $150\,\text{ms}^{-1}$, with what speed does the bullet hit the thief's car (as seen by thief)? According to thief in the car?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A man is going up in a lift (open at the top) moving with a constant velocity 3m/s. He throws a ball up at 5m/sec relative to the lift when the lift is 50 m above the ground. Height of the lift when the ball meets it during its downward journey is ( $g = 10 \text{ m/s}^2$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two bodies start from the same point $A$ and move along a single straight line. At $t=0$, their accelerations are $a$ and $2a$, and their initial velocities are $2u$ and $u$, respectively. When one body catches up with the other, the distance travelled from point $A$ is $\frac{\alpha u^2}{a}$. Here $\alpha$ is an integer. Determine $\alpha$ :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A motorcycle is moving with a velocity $80 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ ahead of a car moving with a velocity of $65 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ in the same direction. What is the relative velocity in $\mathrm{km} / \mathrm{hr}$ of the motorcycle with respect to the car?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A train in moving in the north at a speed $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. Its length is 150 m . A parrot is flying parallel to the train in the south with a speed of $5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Find out the time taken by the parrot to cross the train in seconds.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A 120 m long train A is moving in a straight line with a speed $20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Another 130 m long train B moving with a speed $30 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ in the opposite direction crosses train A in a time
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A stone is projected vertically upward with a speed of $50 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. At the same instant, from the same point, another stone is thrown vertically downward with a speed of $50 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. At the moment when the first stone reaches its maximum height, the velocity of the second stone relative to the first stone is :
questions.subject: Physics
